#### [Hexalith\.EventStore\.Testing](index.md 'index')
### [Hexalith\.EventStore\.Testing\.Compliance](Hexalith.EventStore.Testing.Compliance.md 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Testing\.Compliance')

## TerminatableComplianceAssertions Class

Test\-time helpers that convert the runtime\-only Apply\-method obligations of
[Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/hexalith.eventstore.contracts.aggregates.iterminatable 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable') aggregate states into Tier 1 assertions\.

```csharp
public static class TerminatableComplianceAssertions
```

Inheritance [System\.Object](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.object 'System\.Object') &#129106; TerminatableComplianceAssertions

### Remarks
[Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/hexalith.eventstore.contracts.aggregates.iterminatable 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable') carries a runtime\-only constraint declared in its own
            remarks: any state implementing the interface MUST also declare a no\-op
            `Apply()` method, because
            [Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Events\.AggregateTerminated](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/hexalith.eventstore.contracts.events.aggregateterminated 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Events\.AggregateTerminated') rejection events are persisted to the event
            stream and replayed during actor rehydration\. Domain teams that omit the method
            pass first\-close and first\-rejection tests, then fail only after actor
            deactivation and reactivation when the persisted rejection event replays\.
            This helper turns that latent failure mode into a deterministic Tier 1
            assertion\. Failures throw the same [Hexalith\.EventStore\.Client\.Aggregates\.MissingApplyMethodException](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/hexalith.eventstore.client.aggregates.missingapplymethodexception 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Client\.Aggregates\.MissingApplyMethodException')
            that aggregate rehydration throws at runtime, so test\-time and runtime
            failure ergonomics are identical\.


Recommended: call this from every aggregate state's primary test class, even
when the state does not currently implement [Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/hexalith.eventstore.contracts.aggregates.iterminatable 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable'). The
helper is a no-op for non-terminatable states, so the call activates
automatically the moment the interface is later added.

[Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/hexalith.eventstore.contracts.aggregates.iterminatable 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable') Apply\-method contract: a public instance
`void Apply()` method must exist\.
No\-op for state types that do not implement [Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/hexalith.eventstore.contracts.aggregates.iterminatable 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Contracts\.Aggregates\.ITerminatable')\.

```csharp
    where TState : class;
```
